name: Pollution Monitoring Standards and Thresholds
description: Comprehensive reference of pollution standards and regulatory thresholds for air quality, water quality, soil contamination, and noise, covering WHO, US EPA, and EU standards.

airQuality.description: Ambient air quality standards and guidelines for key pollutants. Thresholds are expressed as concentrations not to be exceeded over specified averaging periods.

pollutants.healthSignificance: Penetrates deep into the lungs and bloodstream, causing cardiovascular disease, respiratory illness, lung cancer, and premature death. No safe threshold identified.

thresholds.note: Proposed revision to 10 ug/m3 under the 2022 Zero Pollution Package

pollutants.healthSignificance: Causes respiratory and cardiovascular morbidity, exacerbates asthma, and contributes to premature mortality.

thresholds.note: Not to be exceeded more than once per year on average over 3 years
Not to be exceeded more than 35 days per year

pollutants.healthSignificance: Respiratory irritant, exacerbates asthma, increases susceptibility to respiratory infections. Precursor to ground-level ozone and secondary PM2.5.

thresholds.note: 100 ppb; 98th percentile of daily 1-hour max, averaged over 3 years
Not to be exceeded more than 18 times per year

pollutants.healthSignificance: Irritant to respiratory system, exacerbates asthma. Precursor to sulphate aerosols (PM2.5) and acid deposition affecting ecosystems.

thresholds.note: 75 ppb; 99th percentile of daily 1-hour max, averaged over 3 years
0.5 ppm; not to be exceeded more than once per year
Not to be exceeded more than 24 times per year
Not to be exceeded more than 3 times per year

pollutants.healthSignificance: Causes respiratory morbidity, reduces lung function, triggers asthma attacks. Damages crops and vegetation. Formed from NOx and VOCs in sunlight.

thresholds.note: 0.070 ppm; annual 4th highest daily max, averaged over 3 years
Not to be exceeded more than 25 days per year averaged over 3 years

pollutants.healthSignificance: Reduces oxygen-carrying capacity of blood (carboxyhaemoglobin formation). At high concentrations causes headache, dizziness, impaired judgement, and death.

thresholds.note: 9 ppm; not to be exceeded more than once per year
35 ppm; not to be exceeded more than once per year

pollutants.healthSignificance: Benzene is a known human carcinogen (Group 1, IARC). Other VOCs cause respiratory irritation and are ozone precursors. No safe level for benzene carcinogenicity.

thresholds.source: WHO Air Quality Guidelines for Europe, 2nd edition (2000)
thresholds.note: Unit risk estimate: 6 x 10^-6 per ug/m3 (lifetime excess risk of leukaemia)
Benzene regulated as Hazardous Air Pollutant (HAP) under Section 112 of Clean Air Act

waterQuality.description: Water quality parameters and regulatory thresholds for surface water discharge and ambient water quality.

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Indicates the amount of dissolved oxygen consumed by biological organisms to decompose organic matter. High BOD depletes dissolved oxygen, causing fish kills and ecosystem degradation.

thresholds.source: EPA recommended water quality criteria for secondary treatment (40 CFR 133)

thresholds.applicability: Urban wastewater treatment plant discharge limit

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Measures total quantity of oxygen required to oxidise all organic and inorganic matter. Broader than BOD; indicates total organic pollution load.

thresholds.applicability: Discharge limit for treatment plants >2,000 population equivalent
General effluent guideline for direct discharge

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Reduces light penetration, smothers benthic organisms, clogs fish gills, and transports bound pollutants.

thresholds.applicability: Monthly average effluent limit for secondary treatment

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Extreme pH values are lethal to aquatic organisms and affect the solubility and toxicity of metals and other pollutants.

thresholds.source: EPA Quality Criteria for Water (Gold Book, 1986)

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Highly toxic heavy metal. Bioaccumulates in aquatic organisms and enters the food chain. Causes kidney damage in humans and is a Group 1 carcinogen (IARC).

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria (2016)
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)
Annual average EQS for inland surface waters (varies by water hardness class)

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Cr(VI) is highly toxic and a known human carcinogen. Cr(III) is less toxic. Both forms affect aquatic organisms at elevated concentrations.

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion for Cr(III) (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)
Drinking water parametric value for chromium (total)

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Essential trace element at low concentrations but toxic to aquatic organisms (especially invertebrates and fish) at elevated levels. Bioaccumulates.

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Neurotoxin that bioaccumulates. Causes neurological and developmental damage in children. Toxic to aquatic organisms at low concentrations.

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)
Annual average EQS for inland surface waters

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Highly toxic neurotoxin. Methylmercury bioaccumulates through the food chain. Damages nervous system, kidneys, and developing foetuses. Subject to Minamata Convention.

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria

thresholds.applicability: Maximum allowable concentration (MAC-EQS) for inland surface waters

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Toxic to aquatic organisms, particularly algae and invertebrates. Can cause dermatitis and respiratory effects in humans. Group 1 carcinogen (nickel compounds).

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)
Annual average EQS for inland surface waters (bioavailable concentration)

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Essential trace element but toxic to aquatic organisms at elevated concentrations, particularly fish and invertebrates.

thresholds.source: EPA National Recommended Water Quality Criteria
thresholds.applicability: Freshwater chronic criterion (hardness-dependent; value at 100 mg/L CaCO3)

thresholds.source: Commonly applied EQS values used by Member States
thresholds.applicability: Varies by Member State; not a priority substance under WFD

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Major cause of eutrophication in water bodies. Excess nitrogen leads to algal blooms, oxygen depletion, and loss of biodiversity.

thresholds.applicability: Discharge limit for sensitive area treatment plants (>100,000 PE); annual mean
General effluent guideline for direct discharge

parameters.ecologicalSignificance: Key limiting nutrient for eutrophication in freshwater systems. Even small increases can trigger algal blooms and ecosystem degradation.

thresholds.applicability: Discharge limit for sensitive area treatment plants (>100,000 PE); annual mean
General effluent guideline for direct discharge
Ambient criteria for lakes and reservoirs (ecoregion-dependent)

soilContamination.description: Soil screening levels and intervention values for key contaminants. Used to assess whether soil quality poses a risk to human health or the environment.

thresholds.applicability: Intervention value (serious contamination level triggering remediation assessment)

thresholds.note: Based on IEUBK model for children; EPA proposed lowering to 100 in 2024

thresholds.applicability: Residential soil screening level (elemental mercury, non-cancer)
EPA uses carbon range fractions (C5-C8, C9-C18, C19-C36) rather than total TPH
thresholds.note: Residential: C9-C18 aliphatics = 1600 mg/kg; C19-C36 aliphatics = 23000 mg/kg

parameters.note: Benzo(a)pyrene is used as a marker for the PAH group; total PAH (sum of 10) Dutch intervention value = 40 mg/kg

thresholds.applicability: Intervention value (sum of 10 PAHs per RIVM)
Provisional intervention value for PFOS in soil (residential function)
Provisional intervention value for PFOA in soil (residential function)

noise.description: Environmental noise guidelines and regulatory thresholds for the protection of human health and well-being.

parameters.description: Weighted 24-hour equivalent noise level with 5 dB penalty for evening (19:00-23:00) and 10 dB penalty for night (23:00-07:00) periods. Used as the primary indicator in the EU Environmental Noise Directive.

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Road traffic noise: strongly recommended not to exceed to reduce health effects

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Railway noise: strongly recommended not to exceed

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Aircraft noise: strongly recommended not to exceed

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Wind turbine noise: conditionally recommended not to exceed
Threshold for strategic noise mapping in agglomerations (indicative)
thresholds.note: Member States set their own limit values; 55 dB Lden is a common action planning threshold

parameters.description: Equivalent continuous noise level during the night period (23:00-07:00). Night-time noise is particularly harmful, causing sleep disturbance, cardiovascular effects, and annoyance.

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Road traffic noise: strongly recommended not to exceed for night-time

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Railway noise: strongly recommended not to exceed for night-time

thresholds.source: WHO Environmental Noise Guidelines for the European Region (2018)
thresholds.applicability: Aircraft noise: strongly recommended not to exceed for night-time

thresholds.source: WHO Night Noise Guidelines for Europe (2009)
thresholds.applicability: General night noise guideline for health protection
thresholds.note: Interim target: 55 dB Lnight for countries unable to meet guideline in short term
Member States set their own values; 50 dB Lnight is a common action planning threshold
